hero.name: Tyrese Mosley
hero.subtitle: Information Science student building real tech, on track for a career in cybersecurity.

about.heading: Who I am
about.bio: My name is Tyrese Mosley, and I am an Information Science student with a passion for creating a meaningful impact in the world. I enjoy transforming complex challenges into clean and intuitive experiences. I also believe it is essential to protect information and ensure safe digital interactions. My goal is to become a cybersecurity analyst in the near future.
about.journey: My journey started with a simple curiosity at a young age about how things work on the internet. That curiosity turned into learning as much as I could, eventually leading me to build real tools for a nonprofit, We Love Philly, in Philadelphia, and making real world experiences and connections. Every project teaches me something new, and every experience is a step toward the kind of career that I want.
about.footer: Philadelphia, PA · Posse Scholar · Information Science

quote.verse: Yet what we suffer now is nothing compared to the glory he will reveal to us later.

projects.title: We Love Philly Youth Tracker
projects.description: Internal student lifecycle tracking platform for a Philadelphia nonprofit. Replaces fragmented spreadsheets and Airtable with a single source of truth, tracking students from application through alumni engagement.

caseStudy.hook: A not only secure, but a fully synced student tracking platform that brought together 3 disconnected tools into one source of truth for over 547+ Philadelphia youth students.
caseStudy.story:
We Love Philly tracks over 547 students across their entire journey, from application to alumni status, but that data lives scattered across Google Classroom, Airtable, and Google Sheets, with no single source of truth. Staff are having to manually cross-reference these three tools just to answer the basic question of how many students are currently active.

As a technology project lead, I own this problem. I mapped out how each system stored and structured its data, and then used Claude Code to build and ship it. I designed the integration so that Airtable runs fully and is able to sync at 2:00 a.m. every night. The Google Classroom syncs grades and attendance by matching students' emails at 4:00 a.m. In Zapier, a Webhook handles the real-time updates from record changes outside the regular working hours. I also was able to design a self-serve flow so that when staff make any new Google Sheets for the cohort's attendance and hours, they will be able to connect seamlessly with the app.

Since this platform handles sensitive student data, I  implemented a two-factor authentication that was restricted to verify We Love Philly staff only. Staff sign in with their email and password and then receive a six-digit code sent to their inbox that must be entered to complete the site sign-in, adding a second layer of protection beyond just credentials. This was my first-hand implication of two-factor authentication directly on a site.

Having this system gives the leadership a live breakdown of every student by stage, from applicant to enrolled to active to interning or alumni. They're all searchable and filtered across all 547 records. That design supports both scheduled and on-demand reporting so staff can generate a report when they need one or receive automated reports on a recurring schedule without lifting a finger. Also, both self-monitoring so that the team can see whether a sync has succeeded, was partially completed, or failed altogether, rather than them having to search and find anything that's wrong.

The hardest part of this wasn't writing the code, it was the system thinking. Mapping how the three tools with different data models, update frequencies, and ownership needed to all come together into one reliable source, securing access to the data appropriately, debugging issues as they surfaced in my production, and working with an AI assistant to develop tools to translate my decisions into working software.

images.caption: Access restricted to verified We Love Philly staff emails only.
Two-factor authentication, a 6-digit code is required to complete every login.
The verification email staff receive on every sign-in attempt.
A live breakdown of all 547+ students by program stage, exportable in one click.
Automated monitoring shows exactly when each data source synced successfully, or failed.
Airtable and Google Classroom sync automatically every night, with real-time updates available via webhook.
Step by step on how to integrate new Google Sheets.

projects.title: WLP PTO Portal
projects.description: Internal PTO management system for nonprofit staff. Features request submission, manager approvals, team calendar with blackout dates, email notifications, and a GitHub-backed CMS.

projects.title: Personal Portfolio
projects.description: This site — built from scratch with Vite + React and plain CSS. Features a GitHub-backed CMS, PIN-protected admin panel, smooth scroll animations, and Intersection Observer-powered reveals.
